content: I'll split this thread-safety design bead into smaller, focused child beads that can be completed sequentially.

args.command:
br create "Document race conditions for concurrent first-failure state" "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Identify and document all possible race conditions when multiple requests attempt to read/write first-failure state concurrently in async FastAPI.

## Scope
- Enumerate specific race scenarios (multiple sends failing simultaneously, read-during-write, write-during-write)
- Document the state transitions that can conflict
- Identify which operations are atomic and which are not

## Acceptance Criteria
- All identified race conditions listed with concrete examples
- Each race condition includes a scenario description and potential impact
- State transition diagram or clear explanation of conflicting operations"
args.description: Create child bead 1: Document race conditions

output:
Exit code 1
Error: error: unexpected argument 'Document race conditions for concurrent first-failure state' found

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

For more information, try '--help'.

## Task

## Scope
- Enumerate specific race scenarios (multiple sends failing simultaneously, read-during-write, write-during-write)
- Document the state transitions that can conflict
- Identify which operations are atomic and which are not

## Acceptance Criteria
- All identified race conditions listed with concrete examples
- Each race condition includes a scenario description and potential impact
- State transition diagram or clear explanation of conflicting operations"
args.description: Create child bead 1: Document race conditions

args.command:
br create --title "Document race conditions for concurrent first-failure state" --description "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Identify and document all possible race conditions when multiple requests attempt to read/write first-failure state concurrently in async FastAPI.

## Scope
- Enumerate specific race scenarios (multiple sends failing simultaneously, read-during-write, write-during-write)
- Document the state transitions that can conflict
- Identify which operations are atomic and which are not

## Acceptance Criteria
- All identified race conditions listed with concrete examples
- Each race condition includes a scenario description and potential impact
- State transition diagram or clear explanation of conflicting operations" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 1: Document race conditions

output: adc-4ol5

args.command:
br create --title "Research async locking mechanisms for Python" --description "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Research and document available locking mechanisms for async Python/FastAPI applications.

## Scope
- asyncio.Lock: usage, behavior, pros/cons
- Module-level mutex patterns for async code
- Atomic operations available in Python (sqlite3 row locking, atomic writes)
- Any FastAPI-specific concurrency patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each locking mechanism documented with code examples
- Pros and cons listed for each approach
- Recommendations for which mechanisms suit different scenarios" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 2: Research locking mechanisms

args.command:
br create --title "Design thread-safety strategy for first-failure state" --description "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Design and document the specific thread-safety approach for first-failure state read/write operations.

## Scope
- Choose locking strategy based on research from child bead adc-4783
- Define exactly which operations need locking
- Design the lock acquisition/release pattern
- Consider edge cases (lock timeout, deadlock prevention)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear recommendation for specific locking strategy
- Detailed explanation of where locks are applied
- Code pattern or pseudo-code showing implementation
- Edge case handling documented" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 3: Design thread-safety strategy

output: adc-41u0

args.command:
br create --title "Document performance implications of locking strategy" --description "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Analyze and document the performance impact of the chosen thread-safety strategy.

## Scope
- Measure or estimate lock contention overhead
- Identify bottlenecks (hot paths that will frequently acquire locks)
- Compare performance vs. correctness trade-offs
- Document any mitigation strategies

## Acceptance Criteria
- Performance analysis with specific metrics or estimates
- Identification of performance-critical code paths
- Documentation of acceptable vs. unacceptable performance impact
- Mitigation strategies if performance is a concern" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 4: Document performance implications

output: adc-4rh3

args.command:
br create --title "Write comprehensive thread-safety design document" --description "## Child of: adc-50ld

## Task
Compile all research and design into a comprehensive thread-safety design document.

## Scope
- Synthesize race conditions (from adc-4ol5)
- Include locking mechanism evaluation (from adc-4783)
- Present the chosen strategy (from adc-41u0)
- Include performance analysis (from adc-4rh3)
- Provide implementation guidance for developers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single cohesive design document covering all aspects
- Clear actionable recommendations
- Code examples where applicable
- Document saved to notes/ with appropriate filename" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 5: Write comprehensive design doc

output: adc-5xuy

content: Now I'll chain the children sequentially and convert the parent to an umbrella:
